entries.definition:
s–t–t as a result of a regressive assimilation between the
sounds د /d/ and س /s/ the cardinal number ست (sitt) (six) and its
derivatives are traditionally classified under root س/ت/ت s–t–t
instead of root س/د/س s–d–s. Of this root, two forms occur eight
times in the Qur’an: ست sitt seven times and ستين sittīn once.

ست sitt [card. num.] six (50:38) ولقد خلقنا السماوات والأرض وما بينهما
في ستة أيام وما مسنا من لغوب We created the heavens, and the earth,
and that in between them in six Days, and no weariness touched
Us.

ستين sittīn [card. no.] sixty (58:4) فمن لم يستطع فإطعام ستين مسكينا
and anyone unable to do this should feed sixty needy persons.
